$comment: MCP servers the IntelliRecon agent connects to. Each is spawned over stdio; failures are non-fatal (the agent still gets its built-in run_command / report_finding tools). Add any MCP server here — the bundled 'engine' below wires in its 150+ security tools once the IntelliRecon Engine API server is running on :8888. 'toolTimeouts' sets per-tool call ceilings in SECONDS ('default' applies to the whole server); slow subdomain/DNS enumerators (amass, fierce, dnsenum) need far more than the 300s global default. A ceiling is a cap, not a wait — a tool returns as soon as it finishes — so headroom is cheap; the operator can always hit Stop to cancel early.
